sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After the third RIGHT, the red blocks changed back to red! Looking at the image, the two blocks on the left are now red again, and they're positioned inside the green frame area. Let me check the segmentation to understand the mechanics better.
The pattern seems to be:
- Red blocks start outside the green frame (left side)
- When I move RIGHT, the character and red blocks move right together
- The blocks change to gray when they're partially overlapping the green frame
- Now after the third RIGHT, they're red again and fully inside the green frame
This suggests the goal is to position the red blocks inside the green frame. Let me check the current state more carefully.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after third RIGHT
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("Current nodes:")
for n in nodes:
    b = n['boundary']
    min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
    max_r = max(p[0] for p in b)
    min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
    max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
    print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min_r}-{max_r}, cols={min_c}-{max_c}")

# Check for level completion
print(f"\nLevel completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
print(f"Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over', False)}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
